title: I think the Moon is a fricken space ship

transcript.text: Okay, hello. This video is, uh, that I think the moon is a frickin spaceship, dude. I know this is going insane, and the membrane, but whatever. I have to, well, I don't know what people say, maybe you don't.

Now, I originally got this idea because I think some guy, I think from the YouTube channel, Jack Baldi, J-O-G-B-H-O-D-I, um, some guy who talked to an alien, that supposedly the alien told him that the moon is actually, um, older than our sun and was dragged here from another galaxy, or another solar system.
Um, and there are definitely some weird things about our moon, man, that I don't, I don't think scientists have, uh, have actually thought through all the way.
Number one, our moon is way too big. It's, uh, almost as big as the Earth. It's like 3,000 miles in diameter.
How did it get so close to our moon, to our, our Earth? If it was that old, by the time it coalesced, as the Earth was coalescing, it, it would have to coalesce into one planet.

So the Earth, so the moon had to have been thrown here. Um, there's another idea that a long time ago, before there was life, split the old Earth in two, and, uh, you know, so now we have the moon and the Earth.
And that's why the, uh, Pacific Ocean is so deep, because that's where the moon was cut out from.
But, if that's true, then why is the composition of the moon so different than the composition of the Earth?
Uh, the moon is coated in, uh, hydrogen powder. Where did this hydrogen powder come from?
Uh, this hydrogen powder stuff is amazing, by the way. It, uh, if you fill one cargo, one space shuttle cargo load full of it,
I think it can power, like, all of the United States for, like, a year or something like this. I don't know the exact statistics, but
If we could figure out how to build a space elevator, um, that would go, I don't know, what is it, 200 miles up in the sky,
Uh, the centrifugal force of the Earth would keep it tight, um, and you would thread it just like a, um, you know, just one thin thread all the way up,
And then you attach another thin thread to it, and you just make it wider and wider, and then you have a space elevator that you can use.
If we had a space elevator, we could cheaply transport moon dust to the Earth, and that would solve all of our, um, energy problems.
Um, so maybe somebody else put more dust on there for us to be able to use later, or for them to use.
Um, it also has a lot of water underneath it, which is explainable, but there's another really strange thing about the moon
That nobody has explained to me, is that the moon is in a geodesic orbiter on the Earth.
That means, you know, that's on one side of the Earth. The moon faces us at all times.
How did that happen? Every, every planetary object that spins, spins in its own rotation.
Actually, how, then, did the moon get in this kind of sink with the Earth? Perfect sink.
Um, the only way I can think is if the moon has one super gigantic, uh, underground, um, volcano
That is filled with iron ore, and so the moon's spinning around, and every time that iron ore comes in line with the Earth,
It pulls toward the Earth, and it just slows, and then it just holds it down.
Kind of like when you spin a bicycle wheel around, it'll eventually settle with a nozzle on the bottom,
Because the gravity pulls the nozzle on the bottom.
Um, but, I mean, as far as my logic tells, for that to be able to happen,
There would have to be one very overwhelmingly strong volcano.
But if you look at a sphere, the volcanoes are all over the place.
They don't come out just on one side.
So, um, I'm thinking somebody deliberately made the moon in a geodesic orbit around the Earth
For the purposes of building cities on the other side of it that we cannot see,
And then going right to the horizon, right, where you can peek over and look at the Earth
And put these giant telescopes.
So when I look up at the moon, I look at this, the whole periphery, I see these big eyeballs looking right at me.
And, uh, the astronauts do say that when they flew around the moon, they saw cities, lighted cities there.
I mean, they wouldn't tell you that now, but I heard that there were some astronauts who said they thought that,
They heard that, they saw that, and that there were actually people who said they saw photos
Of lighted cities on the other side of the moon, um, before NASA got rid of them.
Um, and another weird thing about the moon is that it's hollow.
So, when they did a sound check on it, it rung like a bell, and, uh, nothing would ring and reverberate like a bell
Unless it's hollow, and how could a planet be hollow?
So, I mean, maybe what this guy said is true.
Uh, the moon is a very old planet, older than our sun, from a long ways away.
It died.
And somebody goes, alright, cool, let's grab this thing, let's, uh, refurbish it, and bring it over to this Earth place
So that we can use it as a base to watch these crazy monkey people, um, and film them.
Maybe they could zoom in and film us, and, uh, so that they could show us the footage later.
Maybe we'll get to watch, uh, Julius Caesar getting stabbed from the film that some alien took 2,000 years ago.
Um, so, um, so what do you think?
Is the moon a spaceship?
Is the moon...
I'm not saying it was artificially created.
It could be a planet that just got re-redone and brought from someplace else.
It might make me sound crazy, but it's just a feeling that I have.
Because the universe is so crazy.
There's so many possibilities.
Now, just since I have a couple of minutes left, uh, Richard Hovland did write a book called, um, Dark Mission.
Where he has photos of, uh, supposedly taken from NASA of, uh, domes on the moon.
So it's not just, uh, it's not just cities on the dark side, but also on the light side.
Um, there's photos where you see actually a haze, like a dome haze.
And then you actually see a haze over the whole moon, like it has an atmosphere.
Um, there's a spire on there, supposedly, that's like a couple miles high.
An obelisk.
There's a robot head up there.
Um, lined up there.
Even lattice work from, um, from like some kind of a grid building, supposedly they saw up there.
And, uh, perforated, uh, domes.
So, I don't know.
What do you think?
Am I totally nuts?
Should I be locked up?
Is the moon a spaceship?
Yep.
